name: Developer experience
when_to_use: APIs, SDKs, CLIs, developer docs, examples, setup, errors, local dev, build/test workflows, migrations, package contracts, auth, IDE integration, plugins, dev-facing perf, telemetry — the experience of a developer integrating your product (AI/Agent SDK design and coding-agent readiness moved to the agent-experience skill). A peer discipline to ux (end users) and agent-experience (AI agents) — not a subset of either; route by audience.

name: Documentation experience
when_to_use: documentation as the product surface — README/quickstarts, API references, examples, error copy, help centers, onboarding help, OpenAPI/MCP tool-contract descriptions, doc telemetry, human-vs-agent audience conflicts (agent-readable docs — llms.txt, RAG-friendly structure — moved to the agent-experience skill)

name: Performance & observability
when_to_use: systems performance and observability — latency budgets, throughput/scalability, p99/tail latency, distributed tracing, structured logs, metrics, SLO/error-budget programs, profiling, capacity planning across backend/browser/database tiers

name: Test-suite quality
when_to_use: test-suite review/design/triage/strategy/pruning — unit, integration, e2e/UI, exploratory, property-based, contract, snapshot, mutation, performance tests; flakiness, false-pass risk, brittleness, test-pyramid/trophy decisions

name: UX & accessibility
when_to_use: user-facing product UX and accessibility — usability heuristics, cognitive walkthroughs, forms, navigation/IA, error/recovery copy, onboarding, checkout/signup friction, WCAG/keyboard/screen-reader basics, dark-pattern review. A peer discipline to dx (developers) and agent-experience (AI agents) — not a subset of either; route by audience.

name: UI design craft
when_to_use: creating and polishing visual UI — frontend mockups, dashboards, design systems/tokens, interaction prototypes, motion/effects, slide decks, artifact handoff, anti-slop visual quality. Use this to BUILD the artifact; use ux to AUDIT one

name: Clean architecture
when_to_use: internal code structure — dependency rule, layered/hexagonal/onion boundaries, ports/adapters, DDD tactical and strategic patterns, bounded contexts, anemic-domain detection, refactor sequencing, cross-cutting concerns
